changes_summary: Explicitly bound raw realtime task hydration to the canonical validator/mapper in `src/shared/lib/taskRowMapper.ts`, clarified that store snapshots use canonical `Task` objects rather than `TaskRecord`, and added tests plus boundary rules to prevent partial realtime snapshots or ad hoc mappers.

success_criteria.criterion: A new `src/shared/state/realtimeStore.ts` exists with effective-project task storage, full-record single-task upsert, batch pending-task bootstrap upsert, scoped reset actions, and selector helpers for single-task and pending-generation task views.

Cold-start hydration is explicitly implemented through centralized query or repository helpers so current-project task details and pending-generation badges are correct before the first new realtime event.

Raw realtime task hydration is explicitly bound to the canonical validator/mapper in `src/shared/lib/taskRowMapper.ts`, so store writes use `isTaskDbRow(...)` and `mapTaskDbRowToTask(...)` or an explicitly aligned `TaskDbRow`-compatible type, not an ad hoc `TaskRecord` mapper.

`RealtimeProvider` writes task realtime updates into the store from raw `RealtimeConnection` rows, not from reduced `RealtimeEventProcessor` payloads, while preserving the existing selected-project Supabase connection lifecycle and the public status-only `useRealtime` API.
Incomplete or invalid raw realtime task rows trigger a targeted fetch or React Query prefetch fallback before store seeding, rather than creating partial task snapshots in `realtimeStore`.

`useGetTask` preserves the empty-id disabled contract and `resolveTaskProjectScope()` fallback behavior while reading from the store when available and staying React-Query-authoritative for fetch lifecycle.

Every live single-task cache writer or reader that uses `taskQueryKeys.single(...)` is either integrated into canonical store seeding or explicitly deferred with rationale and test impact, including `useTaskPrefetch`, `generationTaskCache`, `GenerationTaskContext`, and `useVariantSourceTask`.

`usePendingGenerationTasks` preserves current cold-start semantics by bootstrapping the full current-project queued or in-progress task set before deriving generation-specific results from narrow store selectors.

`usePendingGenerationTasks`, `useGenerationTaskDetails`, and other visible current-project single-task surfaces read from narrow store selectors or the adapted single-task path instead of relying on unrelated invalidation churn for updates.
`useTaskStatusCounts` preserves current derivation semantics, including root-task exclusion, visible task-type filtering, processing-status rules, and recent success or failure windows.
`useTaskStatusCounts({ allProjectIds })` remains correct by keeping a query-authoritative freshness path rather than relying on selected-project realtime store updates for non-selected projects.
Current-project `useTaskStatusCounts(projectId)` also remains query-authoritative in M3 unless a full-project bootstrap is added, so existing callers do not regress to partial counts.
Selectors that return objects or arrays provide stable references for semantically unchanged results, using `shallow` or equivalent equality control, and tests cover that behavior.

Store, provider, hook, and facade tests cover task upsert, batch pending bootstrap, scoped reset, selector narrowing for unrelated task updates, empty-id disabled behavior, `resolveTaskProjectScope()` fallback behavior, canonical raw-event hydration with fetch fallback, and coherent store seeding from prefetch, preload, and generation-task facade helpers.
`npx tsc --noEmit` completes successfully.
`npm test` completes at baseline.
Existing public hook and facade signatures remain stable enough that gallery items, variant details, Tasks Pane, lightbox, task details, and generation-task consumers require little or no API churn.
`useActiveTaskClips` is explicitly classified as deferred or migrated with rationale so the milestone scope remains intentional.
Gallery items no longer rerender on unrelated task events in a 50+ item runtime smoke test.
A per-task status surface, if present after migration, rerenders only for the matching task when observed with runtime telemetry.

assumptions: `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts` remains available as a local reference pattern, but `realtimeStore.ts` stays purpose-built around effective-project task snapshots rather than becoming a generic entity abstraction.
`RealtimeProvider` remains the selected-project realtime boundary and `RealtimeConnection` continues subscribing to one selected-project task channel at a time.

Raw realtime task payloads can be treated as untrusted input for store hydration and validated with `isTaskDbRow(...)` before mapping; if the implementation instead aligns realtime typing to `TaskDbRow`, it will still reuse `mapTaskDbRowToTask(...)` as the sole mapper.
React Query remains authoritative for server truth and fetch lifecycle; the store exists only to narrow subscriptions for current-project single-task and pending-generation readers.
Cold-start correctness for pending-generation badges must still come from the existing current-project pending-task query, which seeds the store before later realtime updates arrive.
`useTaskStatusCounts` should remain query-authoritative in M3 because the new store will not own the full historical project snapshot required for recent success and failure windows.
`GenerationTaskContext` remains a facade over generation-task mapping and preload helpers; M3 must keep that facade coherent rather than replacing it.
`useActiveTaskClips` remains outside M3 unless it can adopt an already-built selector helper with no new semantics, no widened scope, and negligible extra tests.
